thesis: Copper faces a structural supply deficit as electrification demand (EVs, renewables, AI data centers, grid modernization) outpaces constrained mine supply. S&P Global projects a 19M tonne shortfall by 2050. Miners with existing production benefit from operating leverage.

drivers: Copper deficit materializes faster than expected
AI data center demand surprises to upside

risks: Trade war tariffs distort copper markets

base_case.assumptions: Steady electrification demand; modest supply growth; copper trades $4.50–5.00/lb; COPX returns 15–20% annually.

catalysts.event: Quarterly copper inventory reports (LME, COMEX, SHFE)
FCX Q2 earnings — watch for copper guidance

key_risks: Political risk in Peru/DRC mining regions
